     The Numbers Business Report

The Hunger Games (2012)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $408,010,692Details
International Box Office $269,912,687
Worldwide Box Office $677,923,379
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $153,381,644
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $72,749,433
Total $226,131,077
Further financial details...

Synopsis

Every year in the ruins of what was once North America, the nation of Panem forces each of its twelve districts to send a teenage boy and girl to compete in the Hunger Games. Part twisted entertainment, part government intimidation tactic, the Hunger Games are a nationally televised event in which “Tributes” must fight with one another until one survivor remains. Pitted against highly-trained Tributes who have prepared for these Games their entire lives, Katniss is forced to rely upon her sharp instincts as well as the mentorship of drunken former victor Haymitch Abernathy. If she’s ever to return home to District 12, Katniss must make impossible choices in the arena that weigh survival against humanity and life against love.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$152,535,747 (37.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.67 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:60.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,137 opening theaters/4,137 max. theaters, 9.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $613,041,195

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 23rd, 2012 (Wide) by Lionsgate
March 23rd, 2012 (IMAX) by Lionsgate
October 15th, 2023 (Special Engagement) by Fathom Events
October 15th, 2023 (IMAX) by Fathom Events
International Releases: March 8th, 2012 (Wide) (Portugal)
March 21st, 2012 (Wide) (Belgium)
March 21st, 2012 (Wide) (France)
March 21st, 2012 (Wide) (Netherlands)
March 21st, 2012 (Wide) (Switzerland)
Video Release: August 18th, 2012 by Lionsgate Home Entertainment
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense violent thematic material and disturbing images—all involving teens.
(Rating bulletin 2212, 2/29/2012)
Running Time: 142 minutes
Franchise: Hunger Games
Cast, crew, or production detail: Ensemble, Intertitle
Film format: IMAX: DMR
Filming location (city or town): Brevard, NC, Charlotte, NC, Henry River Mill Village, NC, Shelby, NC
Filming location (country): United States
Filming location (state or county): Burke County, NC, Cleveland County, NC, Mecklenburg County, NC, North Carolina, United States, Transylvania County, NC
Plot point: Addiction, Class Warfare, Dream Sequence, Forced to Fight to the Death, Government Corruption, Hallucinations, Love Triangle, Non-Chronological, Revenge
Social setting: Dystopia
Source material: Novel or Other Work Adapted by Author, Young Adult Book Adaptation
Subgenre: Action Adventure
Source:Based on Fiction Book/Short Story
Genre:Action
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction
Production/Financing Companies: Lionsgate, Color Force
Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Full financial estimates for this film, including domestic and international box office, video sales, video rentals, TV and ancillary revenue are available through our research services. For more information, please contact us at research@the-numbers.com.


  • Summary
  • Box Office
  • Full Financials

Synopsis

Every year in the ruins of what was once North America, the nation of Panem forces each of its twelve districts to send a teenage boy and girl to compete in the Hunger Games. Part twisted entertainment, part government intimidation tactic, the Hunger Games are a nationally televised event in which “Tributes” must fight with one another until one survivor remains. Pitted against highly-trained Tributes who have prepared for these Games their entire lives, Katniss is forced to rely upon her sharp instincts as well as the mentorship of drunken former victor Haymitch Abernathy. If she’s ever to return home to District 12, Katniss must make impossible choices in the arena that weigh survival against humanity and life against love.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$152,535,747 (37.4% of total gross)
Legs:2.67 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:60.2% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$80,000,000 (worldwide box office is 8.5 times production budget)
Theater counts:4,137 opening theaters/4,137 max. theaters, 9.1 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $613,041,195

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: March 23rd, 2012 (Wide) by Lionsgate
